items.title: Crypto market slips even as equities advance. Pump surges on social-media chatter
items.description: With a Fear and Greed index pointing solidly at "fear," crypto prices drifted lower even as stock index futures rose. PUMP's 20% surge was the only real headline in an otherwise quiet session.

items.title: Hyperliquid plans to add decentralized prediction markets in upgrade to HIP-4
items.description: Permissionless prediction markets will become available first on testnet and later on mainnet, Hyperliquid said.

items.title: Cross-chain protocol Allbridge halts after $1.65 million flash loan exploit
items.description: The attacker used a $1.12 million flash loan from Kamino to manipulate pool ratios, enabling them to withdraw assets at favorable rates before bridging funds.

items.title: U.S. regulatory developments and earnings, ECB rate decision: Crypto Week Ahead
items.description: Your look at what's coming in the week starting July 20.

items.title: Amazon Japan supplier AZ-Com Maruwa to adopt yen stablecoin JPYC for payments
items.description: The Japanese logistics firm plans to pay 2,300 partners, including truck drivers, using JPYC in Japan’s first large-scale corporate stablecoin rollout.

items.title: Live markets: Oil bounce and lingering AI selloff pushes bitcoin under $64,000
items.description: War drives oil up, the Kimi selloff drags chips down, and bitcoin sits between them.

items.title: Moonshot AI IPO push follows Kimi, Alibaba AI releases that shook bitcoin
items.description: Days after Kimi K3 rattled chip stocks, Moonshot is moving toward a Hong Kong listing at a $30 billion-plus valuation while Alibaba's Qwen goes open-weight.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs see new money again, but inflows remain ‘peanuts’ relative to the recent exodus
items.description: Bitcoin ETFs have attracted $273 million in new inflows in two weeks, but the total is barely enough to cover a single "slow" week of recent selling.

items.title: Trader maintains $67K BTC price target: Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
items.description: Bitcoin preserved a key long-term trend line as support for a third week as US-Iran war tensions delivered new five-week highs in oil prices.

items.title: Bitcoin ETF inflows extend to second week, but recovery lacks momentum
items.description: US spot Bitcoin ETFs extended their inflow streak to two weeks with $75.7 million in net inflows, but analysts said demand remains insufficient to fuel a sustained uptrend.

items.title: South Korea eyes September launch for second phase of CBDC pilot: Report
items.description: The Bank of Korea’s CBDC pilot will add two regional banks, new payment features and tests of government subsidy payments using tokenized bank deposits

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Cardano activates van Rossem hard fork
items.description: The upgrade reduces smart contract execution costs while laying the groundwork for Ouroboros Leios, a major scalability upgrade expected later this year.

items.title: Allbridge pauses cross-chain bridge after $1.65M exploit
items.description: The attacker allegedly used a flash loan and rapid swaps to manipulate the bridge’s stablecoin exchange rate.

items.title: Japanese logistics company eyes JPYC stablecoin to pay drivers
items.description: The planned rollout would let thousands of transportation contractors receive digital yen payments more frequently and quickly.

items.title: South Korea probed 40 cases of crypto manipulation over 2 years
items.description: Financial Services Commission Chair Lee Eog-won posted the figures on the second anniversary of the enactment of the Virtual Asset User Protection Act.

items.title: Oil prices erase gains after Iran says U.S. talks could be pursued based on national interests
items.description: American forces have started ninth consecutive night of strikes against Iranian targets.

items.title: Boeing CEO says company still needs 'a couple more years' to repair finances before new jet
items.description: CEO Kelly Ortberg signalled Boeing is focused more on stabilizing its existing business than rushing to develop a successor to its best-selling 737 MAX.

items.title: FIFA emerges as the $9 billion winner as the biggest, brashest World Cup ever draws to a close
items.description: The football governing body presided over the most lucrative sporting event in history and is poised to net over $9 billion in revenue for 2026.

items.title: Bezos backs CuspAI as startup teams up with Nvidia to hunt for chipmaking materials
items.description: CuspAI is part of a new wave of companies looking to use AI to solve challenges in the physical world.

items.title: UK to get a new prime minister as Trump slams Britain as 'Poverty Stricken Disaster'
items.description: The prospect of Burnham, considered more left-leaning than Starmer, replacing the incumbent prime minister sent jitters through bond markets earlier this year.

items.title: Ryanair stock slides 6% as higher fuel costs amid Iran war dent profit
items.description: Ryanair's first-quarter profit took a 34% hit as consumers delayed bookings due to the Middle East crisis, while struggling airlines face a "difficult winter."

items.title: TSMC is accelerating Arizona factory buildout to capitalize on AI 'megatrend,' CFO says
items.description: Speaking in an interview with CNBC, TSMC's Wendell Huang said the fresh investment comes on the back of robust customer demand.

items.title: China's car market heads for worst year since 2021 as sales plunge 20%
items.description: Consumer demand for cars in China tumbles following record-high sales of 23.7 million units in 2025.

items.title: Burnham must remember the first rule of government
items.description: The biggest mistake of all would be to imagine the country enjoys a free fiscal lunch. In fact, it only has hard choices

items.title: Training AI models might be the chance for a workplace power play
items.description: It is becoming clear that the knowledge they require is currently locked inside employees’ heads

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FSt7pOg7G90! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: I will claim Social Security early. Why do so few people talk about the elephant in the room?
items.description: “The strongest argument for claiming benefits earlier goes beyond the traditional break-even analyses.”

items.title: Why it’s time to retire the Magnificent Seven as a stock-market talking point, say these strategists
items.description: Magnificent Seven as a group is underperforming the broader market this year, but strategists at Citi argue it’s no longer a grouping that even makes sense to think about it.

items.title: My husband and I are retired. Our financial adviser, who is in his 30s, called us ‘you guyses.’ Is that unprofessional?
items.description: “I’m wondering whether I should ask to work with someone closer to our generation.”

items.title: Is an opportunity to buy chip stocks nearing? These two big Wall Street banks are divided.
items.description: JPMorgan says a summer buying opportunity is coming for chip stocks, but Morgan Stanley says the sector is in for a hard remainder of 2026.

items.title: Inflation is broadening out, says Goldman economist
items.description: During his Congressional testimony last week, new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh emphasized that one of his primary responsibilities was to ensure that individual price spikes, of which there have been many in recent years, “don’t broaden out.” The bad news is that on the whole they are.

items.title: We have $8 million in traditional IRAs. Should we tap them to buy a house — and take the tax hit?
items.description: “We would rather pay taxes upfront than pay mortgage interest.”

items.title: If history is a guide, there’s still another week before earnings will start to move the stock market
items.description: The S&P 500 is primed to climb during the third, fourth and fifth weeks of the financial reporting period, according to Jefferies.

items.title: Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs sells $55 million Miami mansion he tried to use for bail
items.description: Disgraced music mogul Sean “Diddy” Combs has finally sold the Miami mansion he once put up as collateral in his failed bid for bail in his sex-trafficking case. It was sold for $55 million while he was behind bars.

items.title: Silicon shadows: inside the black market for AI chips | FT Film

items.title: FirstFT: Andy Burnham weighs Number 10 overhaul
items.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Oil rises above $90 and Morgan Stanley takes AI deal crown

items.title: EU fines AliExpress €550mn for failing to prevent sale of illegal goods
items.description: Record penalty under bloc’s Digital Services Act comes amid broader crackdown on Chinese ecommerce platforms

items.title: Bad cabinet appointments will steal Burnham’s precious hours
items.description: Incoming leader would do well to take Rishi Sunak’s advice on maximising your time as prime minister

items.title: Oil touches $90 as Iran hits tankers
items.description: Crude later falls back after Tehran says it has received proposals from mediators about the war

items.title: Segro rejects £13.5bn bid by US rival Prologis in latest takeover twist
items.description: London-listed group declines third offer in escalating battle

items.title: India’s ‘Cockroach’ protests mount on streets of Delhi
items.description: Anger over education system threatens to turn into wider challenge to Narendra Modi’s government

items.title: How the UK can be cleverer about stamp duty
items.description: Complaints about stamp duty are as old as the tax

items.title: China’s ‘national team’ buys shares worth $9bn to prop up market
items.description: State-owned funds announce stock purchases after sharp AI tech sell-off last week

items.title: U.S. Average Gas Price Returns to $4 a Gallon as Iran Crisis Escalates
items.description: A month ago, drivers were enjoying some relief at the pump after the U.S. and Iran signed a deal intended to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Now, prices are rising again.

items.title: Global Oil Price Crosses $90 a Barrel as U.S.-Iran Conflict Widens
items.description: Brent crude, the global benchmark for oil, rose more than 3 percent as trading resumed on Sunday, while S&P 500 futures remained essentially unchanged.

items.title: Nolan’s ‘The Odyssey’ Brings in $124.5 Million at the Box Office on Opening Weekend
items.description: Christopher Nolan’s nearly three-hour epic was No. 1 over the weekend, with about $124.5 million in ticket sales in the United States and Canada.

items.title: The Case of the 3:30 Emails
items.description: Plus: Are you really paying attention to me on Zoom?

items.title: Step Into the ‘Zone of Genius’ (Before A.I. Takes Your Job)
items.description: A decades-old self-help concept is gaining new purchase among people searching for meaningful work in the age of artificial intelligence.

items.title: The ‘Bad Blood’ Between Polymarket’s Shayne Coplan and Kalshi’s Tarek Mansour
items.description: Shayne Coplan of Polymarket and Tarek Mansour of Kalshi are young billionaires intent on dominating prediction markets. It’s more than a battle. It’s “bad blood.”

items.title: Adidas Hopes Its World Cup Moment Can Drive U.S. Sales
items.description: Nearly everything on the field on Sunday will bear the German company’s logo. Bjorn Gulden, the chief executive, says he “couldn’t have scripted it better.”

items.title: Madison Square Garden Sues Wired Magazine Over L.G.B.T.Q. Tracking Report
items.description: The Manhattan venue claims a July article from the technology magazine falsely implied that it tracked information about some celebrities’ sexual orientation for “discriminatory purposes.”
